  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Differential pressure (DP) impulse lines are sloped incorrectly for the service type.",
    "Gas taps are located below the pipe centerline, risking liquid accumulation in the lines.",
    "Liquid taps are located on top of the pipe, risking gas/air pocket entrapment in the lines."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-route and re-slope the impulse lines according to the process fluid requirements.",
    "Relocate gas taps to the top or upper 45 degrees of the process pipe.",
    "Relocate liquid taps to the side or lower 45 degrees of the process pipe."
  ],
  "rationale": "The DP transmitter impulse lines are installed backwards for their respective services. Gas lines must drain back into the process pipe (taps on top), and liquid lines must allow gas bubbles to vent back into the process pipe (taps on the side/bottom). This incorrect slope and tap orientation will cause measurement errors, liquid pockets in gas lines, or gas pockets in liquid lines, violating API RP 551 and manufacturer installation guidelines.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 551 (Process Measurement Instrumentation)",
    "Manufacturer Installation Manuals for DP Transmitters"
  ]
}
